name: python3-puremagic
title: python3-puremagic
summary: pure-Python module to identify files by their magic signature
description:
pure-Python module to identify files by their magic signature
Puremagic is a pure-Python module to identify files by their magic numbers.
It is fast, lightweight, minimalistic, cross-platform, and doesn't have
dependencies. It can serve as a stand-in for python-magic, but its own
magic_file() and magic_string() function are more powerful and also display
a confidence score and possible duplicate matches.

commands.name: puremagic
commands.description: pure-Python utility to identify a file based off its magic numbers

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use puremagic because pure-Python module to identify files by their magic signature.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use puremagic as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `puremagic  is a pure python file identification module. It looks for matching magic numbers in` as documented upstream for puremagic.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair puremagic with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `puremagic --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
